title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent Go integration team receives different topology, traffic, schema, tenant, dependency, and shutdown constraints and transfers the module into a new a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads after bounded decoding with required fields, semantic checks, collection limits, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go messaging team handles case 7650 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Buffer Decoding Boundary, keep Buffer Decoding Boundary connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.
